testcase.name: editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro

failure.message:
Expecting message to be: "Já existe um funcionário cadastrado com este CPF!" but was: " Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:111) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class."
Throwable that failed the check:
org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:111) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.lambda$editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro$4(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:113) at org.assertj.core.api.ThrowableAssert.catchThrowable(ThrowableAssert.java:63) at org.assertj.core.api.AssertionsForClassTypes.catchThrowable(AssertionsForClassTypes.java:892) at org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.catchThrowable(Assertions.java:1366) at org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy(Assertions.java:1210) at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:113) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DirectMethodHandleAccessor.invoke(DirectMethodHandleAccessor.java:103) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580) at org.junit.platform.commons.util.ReflectionUtils.invokeMethod(ReflectionUtils.java:728) at org.junit.jupiter.engine.execution.MethodInvocation.proceed(MethodInvocation.java:60) at org.junit.jupiter.engine.execution.InvocationInterceptorChain$ValidatingInvocation.proceed(InvocationInterceptorChain.java:131) at testcase.failure:
org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError:
Expecting message to be: "Já existe um funcionário cadastrado com este CPF!" but was: " Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:111) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class."
Throwable that failed the check:
org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmJaExistenteLancaErro(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:111) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) at testcase.name: salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado

failure.message:
Expecting message to be: "Já existe um funcionário cadastrado com este CPF!" but was: " Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.salvar(FuncionarioService.java:39) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:63) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class."
Throwable that failed the check:
org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.salvar(FuncionarioService.java:39) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:63) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.salvar(FuncionarioService.java:39) at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.lambda$salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado$1(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:66) at org.assertj.core.api.ThrowableAssert.catchThrowable(ThrowableAssert.java:63) at org.assertj.core.api.AssertionsForClassTypes.catchThrowable(AssertionsForClassTypes.java:892) at org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.catchThrowable(Assertions.java:1366) at org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy(Assertions.java:1210) at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:65) at testcase.failure:
org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError:
Expecting message to be: "Já existe um funcionário cadastrado com este CPF!" but was: " Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.salvar(FuncionarioService.java:39) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:63) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class."
Throwable that failed the check:
org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.salvar(FuncionarioService.java:39) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.salvarRejeitaCpfDuplicado(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:63) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). testcase.name: editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmLivreSalva

error.message: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'existsByCpf' method: funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "52998224725" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioService.editar(FuncionarioService.java:65) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. funcionarioRepository.existsByCpf( "529.982.247-25" ); -> at com.empresa.funcionarios.service.FuncionarioServiceTest.editarComCpfAlteradoParaUmLivreSalva(FuncionarioServiceTest.java:124) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class.
